figures.caption: FIG. 2. Real-space spin densities in the ribbon for different transport energies, corresponding to the propagation of 20 modes (a) and 2 modes (b) for the reflection symmetric ribbon. In the right panel the spin polarization in the asymmetric ribbon is displayed for two different energies corresponding to 2 (c) and 3 (d) propagating modes.
FIG. 3. Spin resolved edge states for both top-bottom symmetric (a) and asymmetric (b) ribbons. The energy is tuned such that only a single transport mode is enabled.
FIG. 1. A sketch of the investigated ribbon with symmetric (a) and antisymmetric (b) edge terminations. The corresponding ribbon band structures are shown.
FIG. 5. The real-space spin densities in the symmetric ribbon configuration with broken translational symmetry is shown for E = −2.8γ (E = −2.75γ) in (a) and (b) respectively.

FIG. 4. Spin current distribution in the ribbon for the edge states transport considered in Fig. 3. The edge spin currents within the asymmetric (symmetric) ribbons are shown in (a) and (b) respectively.
